title: ipv6_addr.c:170-212 snprintf error handling is correct but over-commented
description: lichen_ipv6_addr_to_str() has 15 lines of error handling for a fixed-format snprintf that will never fail in practice (encoding failure, truncation). The checks are correct but the comments explaining what 'ret' means (lines 198, 206-207) restate what the code already expresses. Either trim comments to intent-level ('Handle snprintf failure modes') or trust that the reader knows snprintf semantics.
